title: Oven Cavity Thermistor Open/Short
shortDescription: The microwave oven cavity temperature sensor has an open or short circuit, preventing temperature monitoring.
fullDescription:
Error code F-1 on LG microwaves indicates that the oven cavity thermistor (temperature sensor) has malfunctioned. This sensor monitors the internal temperature of the microwave cavity to prevent overheating and to control sensor cooking programs.

The thermistor can fail due to age, heat exposure, or physical damage. An open circuit (broken sensor) or short circuit will trigger F-1. The sensor is typically located inside the cavity wall and connects to the main control board via a wire harness.

Without a functioning cavity sensor, the microwave cannot regulate temperature for sensor-based cooking and cannot detect overheating conditions, making it unsafe to operate.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave for 2 minutes, then plug it back in. If F-1 returns, the sensor needs testing.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: WARNING: Microwave ovens contain high-voltage capacitors that can hold a lethal charge even when unplugged. Only proceed if you are trained in microwave repair. Disconnect the thermistor wires and measure resistance. At room temperature it should read approximately 50-60 kOhms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is out of range, install a replacement. Route the wiring exactly as the original.

fixSteps.title: Reassemble and test
fixSteps.instruction: Reassemble the microwave, plug it in, and test with a cup of water for 1 minute.

whenToCallPro: Microwave repair involves high-voltage components. Unless you are experienced with microwave electrical safety, always call a professional.

faq.q: Is it safe to repair a microwave myself?
faq.a: Microwave ovens contain a high-voltage capacitor that can hold a lethal charge even when unplugged. Only trained technicians should open the cabinet.

faq.q: How much does a microwave thermistor cost?
faq.a: LG microwave thermistors typically cost $10–$20 for the part.

title: Humidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The humidity sensor used for automatic sensor cooking has malfunctioned.
fullDescription:
Error code F-2 indicates a fault with the microwave's humidity sensor. LG microwaves with sensor cooking use a humidity sensor inside the cavity to detect steam released by food during cooking. The sensor determines when food is done by measuring moisture levels in the air.

The sensor can fail due to grease and food splatter buildup, or from electrical degradation. A dirty sensor often reads incorrectly before failing completely. Regular cleaning of the microwave interior helps prevent this issue.

Manual cooking modes (time and power level) still work with F-2. Only the automatic sensor cooking functions are affected.

causes.cause: Grease and food residue coating the humidity sensor

symptoms: Sensor cooking overcooks or undercooks food
Error appears when selecting sensor cook

fixSteps.title: Clean the microwave interior
fixSteps.instruction: Heat a bowl of water with lemon juice for 3 minutes to create steam that loosens grease. Wipe the entire interior including the ceiling where the humidity sensor is located.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave for 2 minutes and plug it back in. Try a sensor cook program.

fixSteps.title: Run a ventilation cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run the microwave empty for 2 minutes at low power to evaporate any remaining moisture on the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Professional sensor replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve F-2, the humidity sensor needs replacement by a technician.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not fix the sensor, replacement requires opening the microwave cabinet, which should be done by a professional.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-2 humidity sensor error fix

faq.q: Can I cook manually with F-2?
faq.a: Yes. Manual time and power settings work normally. Only automatic sensor cooking is affected.

faq.q: How do I prevent F-2 errors?
faq.a: Clean the microwave interior regularly, especially the ceiling area. Use a microwave cover over food to reduce splatter.

title: Touchpad or Membrane Short Circuit
shortDescription: The touchpad membrane has a short circuit or a stuck key has been detected.
fullDescription:
Error code F-3 indicates that the control board has detected a short circuit or continuously pressed key from the touchpad membrane. The touchpad is a thin flexible circuit behind the front panel that registers button presses when you touch the corresponding area.

Moisture from cooking steam, cleaning spray that seeped behind the panel, or simple wear and tear of the membrane can cause keys to short together. The control board detects this as an impossible combination of simultaneous key presses or a key held for too long.

A power reset may temporarily fix the issue, but if moisture is trapped behind the panel, it will return. The touchpad or the control board may need replacement.

causes.cause: Moisture behind the touchpad from steam or cleaning
Worn touchpad membrane with internal short
Faulty control board key matrix input

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave for 5 minutes to allow moisture to begin evaporating and any static charges to dissipate.

fixSteps.title: Dry the touchpad area
fixSteps.instruction: If steam or moisture is suspected, leave the microwave unplugged with the door open for several hours to let the area behind the touchpad dry out.

fixSteps.title: Test after drying
fixSteps.instruction: Plug in the microwave and test all buttons. If F-3 is gone, avoid spraying cleaning products near the control panel.

fixSteps.title: Replace the touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: If F-3 persists, the touchpad membrane needs replacement. This requires opening the front panel — on microwaves this should be done by a qualified technician.

whenToCallPro: If drying does not resolve the issue, touchpad replacement inside a microwave should be done by a qualified technician due to high-voltage components.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-3 touchpad error fix

faq.q: Why does steam cause touchpad errors?
faq.a: Cooking steam can enter the gap between the touchpad and the front panel. Moisture on the membrane causes false key signals.

faq.q: How do I prevent F-3?
faq.a: Avoid spraying cleaners directly on the control panel. Wipe with a barely damp cloth. Open the door briefly after steamy cooking to vent moisture.

title: Inverter Board Failure
shortDescription: The microwave inverter board has malfunctioned, preventing the magnetron from operating correctly.
fullDescription:
Error code F-5 indicates a failure of the inverter board in LG inverter microwaves. The inverter board controls the power supplied to the magnetron (the component that generates microwaves). Unlike traditional microwaves that cycle the magnetron on and off, inverter microwaves vary the power level continuously for more even cooking.

When the inverter board fails, the microwave cannot generate microwaves at any power level. This is a component-level failure that typically requires board replacement.

Inverter boards can fail due to power surges, component aging, or overheating from blocked ventilation. The inverter board is located inside the microwave cabinet near the magnetron.

causes.cause: Power surge damage to inverter circuit
Loose wiring at inverter board connectors

symptoms: Microwave runs but does not heat food

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave for 5 minutes and plug it back in. A transient glitch may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the microwave vents are not blocked. Over-the-range models need clear vent openings above and behind. Blocked vents cause the inverter board to overheat.

fixSteps.title: Test with water
fixSteps.instruction: Place a cup of water in the microwave and run for 1 minute on high. If the water does not warm, the inverter is not generating power.

fixSteps.title: Professional inverter board replacement
fixSteps.instruction: The inverter board must be replaced by a qualified technician. This involves working inside the microwave cabinet with high-voltage components.

whenToCallPro: Inverter board replacement always requires a professional due to high-voltage safety concerns inside the microwave.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-5 inverter board error fix

faq.q: Is it worth repairing a microwave with F-5?
faq.a: If the microwave is relatively new, inverter board replacement ($90-$150 for the part) is worthwhile. For older units, replacement may be more cost-effective.

faq.q: What is an inverter microwave?
faq.a: Inverter microwaves deliver a continuous stream of power at the selected level, rather than cycling full power on and off. This provides more even cooking.

title: Magnetron Overcurrent Error
shortDescription: The magnetron is drawing excessive current, indicating a possible magnetron failure.
fullDescription:
Error code F-6 indicates that the magnetron — the component that generates microwave energy — is drawing more current than the rated limit. The control board monitors magnetron current and triggers F-6 when it exceeds the safe threshold.

A failing magnetron with internal arcing or shorted filaments is the most common cause. A faulty high-voltage capacitor or diode in the magnetron power circuit can also cause overcurrent conditions. This is a serious failure that requires professional repair.

Do not attempt to service the magnetron or its power circuit yourself. The high-voltage capacitor stores enough energy to cause lethal shock even when the microwave is unplugged.
symptoms: Microwave shuts off shortly after starting
Loud buzzing or arcing sound during operation
Burning smell from inside the microwave

fixSteps.title: Stop using the microwave
fixSteps.instruction: If you hear arcing or smell burning, stop using the microwave immediately and unplug it.

fixSteps.title: Check for visible damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the cavity for burn marks, especially on the waveguide cover (the small panel on the interior wall where microwaves enter). A damaged waveguide cover can cause arcing.

fixSteps.title: Replace the waveguide cover if damaged
fixSteps.instruction: If the waveguide cover has burn marks or holes, it can be replaced without opening the cabinet. Peel or unscrew the old cover and install a new one.

fixSteps.title: Professional magnetron diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If the waveguide cover is fine, the magnetron or its power supply components need professional testing and replacement.

whenToCallPro: Any work inside the microwave cabinet near the magnetron, capacitor, or diode requires a professional technician.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-6 magnetron error fix

faq.q: What is a waveguide cover?
faq.a: It is a small mica or plastic panel inside the microwave cavity that protects the magnetron opening. Food splatter can damage it and cause arcing.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing a magnetron?
faq.a: Magnetron replacement costs $100–$250 with labor. For microwaves over 5 years old, buying a new unit may be more economical.

title: Turntable Motor Error
shortDescription: The turntable motor is not rotating or is not detected by the control board.
fullDescription:
Error code F-7 indicates that the turntable motor is not operating. The turntable rotates food during cooking to ensure even microwave exposure. While some LG models will still cook without turntable rotation, others display F-7 and halt operation.

The turntable motor is a small synchronous motor underneath the microwave floor. It can fail due to age, food spills dripping through the turntable opening, or a jammed turntable mechanism. An overloaded turntable (very heavy dish) can also stall the motor.

The turntable motor is one of the easiest microwave parts to replace because it is accessible from the bottom of the unit without opening the main cabinet.

causes.cause: Food or debris jamming the turntable ring

symptoms: Humming from under the microwave floor

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the turntable plate and support ring. Clean any food debris from the turntable well and the roller ring track.

fixSteps.title: Test without the plate
fixSteps.instruction: Place the support ring back (without the plate) and start the microwave for a few seconds. Watch if the coupler (the three-pronged piece) rotates.

fixSteps.title: Replace the turntable motor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave. The turntable motor is accessible from the bottom panel (countertop models) or back panel. Remove 2-3 screws, disconnect the motor wires, and install the replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the repair
fixSteps.instruction: Reinstall the turntable ring and plate. Run the microwave with a cup of water and verify the turntable rotates smoothly.

whenToCallPro: The turntable motor is safe to replace yourself as it is outside the high-voltage cabinet. Call a pro only if you are unsure about accessing it.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-7 turntable motor error fix

faq.q: Can I use the microwave without the turntable spinning?
faq.a: You can, but food will cook unevenly. Manually rotating the dish halfway through helps.

faq.q: Is the turntable motor safe to replace myself?
faq.a: Yes. The turntable motor is located outside the high-voltage section and is safe to access. Just make sure the microwave is unplugged.

title: Exhaust Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The exhaust or ventilation fan motor is not running or not reaching the expected speed.
fullDescription:
Error code F-8 indicates a problem with the exhaust fan motor, typically found in over-the-range (OTR) LG microwave models. The exhaust fan vents cooking fumes and steam from the cooktop below to the outside or recirculates through a charcoal filter.

The fan motor may have failed, the fan blade may be jammed, or grease buildup on the fan has increased resistance beyond what the motor can handle. Over time, kitchen grease accumulates on the fan and motor, reducing performance.

Counter top microwaves do not typically have this error, as it relates to the ventilation function of OTR models.

causes.cause: Grease buildup on exhaust fan blades and housing

symptoms: Exhaust fan does not turn on
Loud grinding or rattling from fan area

fixSteps.title: Clean the grease filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the grease filters from the bottom of the microwave. Soak them in hot soapy water or run them through the dishwasher. Heavily clogged filters restrict airflow.

fixSteps.title: Check the charcoal filter
fixSteps.instruction: If your microwave recirculates air (no outside vent), the charcoal filter may be saturated. These cannot be cleaned and must be replaced every 6-12 months.

fixSteps.title: Listen to the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Turn on the exhaust fan. If you hear humming but no airflow, the fan blade may be loose or broken. If silence, the motor may be dead.

fixSteps.title: Replace the exhaust fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: This requires removing the microwave from the wall mount to access the internal fan. Due to the weight and wiring involved, professional installation is recommended.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor needs replacement, the microwave must be unmounted from the wall, which typically requires two people and professional wiring knowledge.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-8 exhaust fan error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the grease filter?
faq.a: Monthly for regular cooking use. The aluminum mesh filters can be washed in the dishwasher.

faq.q: Does my over-the-range microwave vent outside?
faq.a: Check your installation. Some are ducted to the outside, others recirculate air through a charcoal filter. Look for a vent duct behind the unit.

title: Door Switch Error
shortDescription: One or more door interlock switches are not responding correctly when the door is opened or closed.
fullDescription:
Error code F-9 indicates a door switch malfunction. LG microwaves use multiple door interlock switches (typically 3) as a safety system. These switches must open and close in the correct sequence when the door is operated. If the control board detects that the switches are not in the expected state, F-9 is generated.

The switches can fail mechanically after thousands of open/close cycles. Slamming the door accelerates wear. A misaligned door latch that does not fully engage the switches can also trigger this error. In some cases, a stuck or broken door latch hook is the root cause.

Door switches are a critical safety component — they prevent the microwave from operating with the door open, which would expose users to harmful microwave radiation.

causes.cause: Worn or failed door interlock switch

symptoms: Microwave does not start when door is closed
Microwave light does not turn on/off when door opens/closes
Door does not feel like it latches firmly

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the door slowly, watching the latch hooks. They should engage smoothly into the switch housing. Check for broken or bent hooks.

fixSteps.title: Clean the latch area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the door latch hooks and the receiving slots in the frame with a damp cloth to remove any food debris that might prevent full engagement.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 2 minutes, then try again. If F-9 persists, the switches need testing.

fixSteps.title: Professional switch replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Door interlock switches are inside the microwave cabinet near the high-voltage components. Replacement must be done by a qualified technician who can safely discharge the capacitor.

whenToCallPro: Door switch replacement requires working inside the microwave cabinet. Always have a professional perform this repair.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-9 door switch error fix

faq.q: Are door switches a safety concern?
faq.a: Yes. Door interlock switches prevent microwave radiation exposure. Do not attempt to bypass them or use the microwave if they are failing.

faq.q: Can slamming the door cause F-9?
faq.a: Yes. Forceful closing accelerates wear on the latch hooks and switches. Always close the microwave door gently.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal communication or memory error.
fullDescription:
Error code F-10 indicates an internal control board malfunction. The main PCB has detected a communication error between its processor and memory, or a checksum error in its firmware. This is typically caused by power fluctuations, static discharge, or component degradation on the board itself.

A simple power reset resolves F-10 in many cases, as the processor reinitializes its memory on startup. If the error returns repeatedly, the control board has a hardware fault and needs replacement.

Power surges from nearby lightning strikes, faulty wiring, or compressor starts on the same circuit can cause the voltage glitches that trigger F-10.

causes.cause: Transient power fluctuation corrupting board memory

symptoms: Display may go blank or show garbled text
Random button presses are not registered

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave for 5 minutes. This allows the control board to fully discharge and reinitialize. Plug back in and test.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply quality
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the microwave is on a dedicated circuit. Avoid sharing the outlet with high-draw appliances that cause voltage dips.

fixSteps.title: Install a surge protector
fixSteps.instruction: If F-10 recurs after power fluctuations or storms, use a surge protector on the microwave outlet.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If F-10 keeps returning despite stable power, the control board needs professional replacement.

whenToCallPro: If a power reset does not permanently resolve F-10, the control board needs replacement by a qualified technician.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-10 control board error fix

faq.q: Is F-10 a serious error?
faq.a: Often F-10 is a one-time glitch resolved by a power reset. It is only serious if it recurs frequently, indicating board failure.

faq.q: Can a power surge destroy the control board?
faq.a: Yes. A strong power surge can permanently damage the microprocessor or memory chips on the board.

title: Microwave Overheating Protection
shortDescription: The microwave has overheated and the thermal protection has shut down the unit.
fullDescription:
Error code F-H indicates that the microwave's internal thermal protection has been activated due to excessive heat. The thermal cutoff switch or thermostat has detected that the internal temperature of the microwave cabinet has exceeded the safe limit.

This commonly occurs when ventilation is blocked (items placed on top of the microwave, restricted vent openings on OTR models), during extended cooking at high power, or when the cooling fan has failed. Cooking with empty cavity or metallic objects can also cause rapid overheating.

The microwave will not restart until the internal temperature drops below the reset threshold. This may take 30-60 minutes. If F-H appears frequently, the cooling system needs attention.
symptoms: Microwave shuts off during or after cooking
Microwave cabinet exterior is very hot to touch

fixSteps.title: Let the microwave cool
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the microwave and open the door. Wait at least 30-60 minutes for the internal temperature to drop.

fixSteps.title: Clear ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Remove any items placed on top of or near the microwave that could block vent openings. For OTR models, check that the vent duct is not crushed or blocked.

fixSteps.title: Test after cooling
fixSteps.instruction: Plug in the microwave and try a short 30-second test with a cup of water. If it works, the overheating was caused by blocked vents or extended use.

fixSteps.title: Check the cooling fan
fixSteps.instruction: During operation, listen for the internal cooling fan. It should run whenever the microwave is on. If you do not hear it, the cooling fan motor may have failed and needs professional replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the cooling fan has failed, or if F-H keeps occurring despite good ventilation and normal use, professional diagnosis is needed.
videoSearchQuery: LG microwave F-H overheating error fix

faq.q: How long do I need to wait before restarting after F-H?
faq.a: At least 30 minutes with the door open and the unit unplugged. The thermal cutoff auto-resets once the temperature drops.

faq.q: Can I put things on top of my microwave?
faq.a: No. Placing items on top blocks ventilation openings and can cause overheating. Keep the top of the microwave clear.
